name: Yunlin County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the bright future of the county
the rice paddies and agricultural plain of central western Taiwan
the Taiwan Strait coast and the rivers that water the plain
the warmth of the people and the vitality of agriculture

description: A white field charged at the centre with a multicoloured emblem combining stylised forms of clouds and forest with the characters 雲林 (Yunlin, 'Cloud Forest').
history: Yunlin County (Yunlin Xian) is an agricultural county on Taiwan's central west coast. The county's name 雲林 literally means 'Cloud Forest', and the current county flag and emblem reflect this poetic name with stylised cloud and forest motifs together with agricultural symbolism.

features.symbolism: stylised forms of clouds and forest with the characters 雲林 ('Cloud Forest'), reflecting the poetic name and the agricultural plain of the county
